prs.title: [ROCm] Enable SimpleCPUOffloadConnector on ROCm
prs.author: hongxiayang
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
Fix https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/40397

Enable `SimpleCPUOffloadConnector`  on ROCm backend.

## Test Plan
Using the upstream nightly docker as the base (where rocm is v7.2.1) and build vllm from source.

(1) unit test:
```
vllm/tests/v1/simple_kv_offload# pytest test_scheduler.py 
```
(2) integration test:
```
vllm/tests/v1/simple_kv_offload# pytest test_integration.py
 ```

prs.title: [Core] Add `VLLM_GPU_SYNC_CHECK` env var
prs.author: njhill
prs.body_head:
vLLM now uses asynchronous scheduling by default and in the majority of cases. Performance relies on the absence of any gpu<->cpu synchronizations on the main cuda stream, but such syncs can be opaque and it is easy for them to creep in accidentally.

This change adds a `VLLM_GPU_SYNC_CHECK` which enables `torch.cuda.set_sync_debug_mode` for the model forward pass and sampler, so that we can easily check for such syncs.

I'm trying first to enable it globally in the CI but we can narrow it if this causes problems (it will be automatically disabled if async scheduling isn't enabled).

prs.title: [LoRA] MoE LoRA Refactor
prs.author: jeejeelee
prs.body_head:
## Motivation


Currently, MoE LoRA is wired in by monkey-patching methods on the modular kernel at construction time. `FusedMoEWithLoRA._inject_lora_into_fused_moe` wraps `FusedMoEKernel.apply`, `TritonExperts.activation`, and `TritonExperts.moe_sum` with `fwd_decorator` / `act_decorator` / `moe_sum_decorator`, and smuggles tensors between them through  `moe_state_dict`. This has several concrete problems:

### 1. Hacky and hard to maintain.
The LoRA contribution is hidden inside decorators on base-MoE methods. Someone reading the MoE experts code sees `self.activation(...)` and `self.moe_sum(...)` — they have no syntactic hint that these calls may actually run LoRA shrink/expand GEMMs, or that  `moe_state_dict` carries cross-call state between them. Debugging requires holding the patching order in your head.

### 2. MoE changes don't see LoRA
 Because the LoRA path lives outside the expert `apply()` functions, any refactor of `TritonExperts` / `UnfusedOAITritonExperts` / `MarlinExperts` has to re-discover the LoRA contract (what `activation` receives, what `moe_sum` receives, what shape `intermediate_cache*` has). Every MoE-side change risks breaking LoRA  silently.

### 3. Hard to extend
  Adding support for new features — EP, additional quantized backends, new expert impls — means replicating or working around the decorator chain, and the state-dict plumbing assumes one specific control flow. There is no extension point for an expert that wants to apply LoRA at a different point .

## Change


Treat LoRA as a `first-class` concern in the modular MoE kernel rather than an external patch.

### 1. `MoELoRAContext`: a single explicit payload

A new `MoELoRAContext` dataclass (`vllm/lora/lora_context.py`) packages all of
the LoRA state a MoE forward pass needs:

- `w13_lora_a_stacked` / `w13_lora_b_stacked` / `w2_lora_a_stacked` /
  `w2_lora_b_stacked`
- `adapter_enabled`, `max_loras`
- routing/sharding info: `top_k`, `w13_num_slices`

prs.title: Refactor move experts
prs.author: Jackmin801
prs.body_head:
## Summary

Moves expert kernel files from the fused_moe/ root into the fused_moe/experts/ subdirectory to reduce clutter and organize expert implementations into their own subfolder:
- cutlass_moe.py → experts/cutlass_moe.py                                                                                              
- flashinfer_cutlass_moe.py → experts/flashinfer_cutlass_moe.py
- fused_marlin_moe.py → experts/marlin_moe.py                                                                                          
- rocm_aiter_fused_moe.py → experts/rocm_aiter_moe.py
- xpu_fused_moe.py → experts/xpu_moe.py                                                                                                
- TritonExperts / TritonWNA16Experts classes extracted from fused_moe.py → experts/triton_moe.py (utility functions like fused_experts, get_moe_configs, try_get_optimal_moe_config remain in fused_moe.py) 

## Test plan

- [x] `pytest -vs tests/kernels/moe`

> AI assistance was used to generate this change.

prs.title: [ROCm] [Wheel] [Bugfix] [Critical] Remove any packages installed from github from rocm.txt e.g  `fastsafetensors` as it is incompatible with `uv pip`
prs.author: tjtanaa
prs.body_head:
## Purpose

Remove any packages installed from github from rocm.txt e.g  `fastsafetensors` as it is incompatible with `uv pip`

Fix issue mentioned in https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/39378#issuecomment-4288042919

```
(vLLM) root@kittyzero:~# uv pip install vllm --pre --upgrade --extra-index-url https://wheels.vllm.ai/rocm/nightly/rocm721
Using Python 3.12.13 environment at: miniconda3/envs/vLLM
× Failed to resolve dependencies for vllm (v0.19.2rc1.dev70+g3975eb6de.rocm721)
╰─▶ Package fastsafetensors was included as a URL dependency. URL dependencies must be expressed as direct requirements or
constraints. Consider adding fastsafetensors @ git+https://github.com/foundation-model-stack/fastsafetensors.git@0.2.2 to your
dependencies or constraints file.
```

## Test Plan

## Test Result

prs.title: [ROCm] Support non-causal attention in ROCM_ATTN
prs.author: micah-wil
prs.body_head:
Fix DFlash spec decoding with ROCM_ATTN by adding support for bidirectional attention over query tokens. This issue was exposed after https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/38300/changes added `test_dflash_speculators_model`, which fails on ROCm before this PR. The default behavior of ROCM_ATTN should remain unchanged with this PR

Since I swapped the FlashAttentionMetadata import in rocm_attn with RocmAttentionMetadata, I had to also swap it in rocm_aiter_unified_attn to make precommit happy. That is the only reason this PR touches that backend.
